input.content: I’m planning a trip that starts from Rivermist, and I need to see if my current fuel situation will get me there. Could you work out the distance?

tools.name: activateParkingBrake
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Activates the parking brake of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

_parkingBrakeForce.description: The force applied to the brake in Newtons.

tools.name: adjustClimateControl
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Adjusts the climate control of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

temperature.description: The temperature to set in degree. Default to be celsius. This is a float type value.

fanSpeed.description: The fan speed to set from 0 to 100. Default is 50.

currentTemperature.description: The current temperature set in degree Celsius.

tools.name: check_tire_pressure
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Checks the tire pressure of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tirePressure.description: The tire pressure of the vehicle.

frontLeftTirePressure.description: The pressure of the front left tire in psi.

frontRightTirePressure.description: The pressure of the front right tire in psi.

rearLeftTirePressure.description: The pressure of the rear left tire in psi.

rearRightTirePressure.description: The pressure of the rear right tire in psi.

healthy_tire_pressure.description: True if the tire pressure is healthy, False otherwise.

tools.name: displayCarStatus
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Displays the status of the vehicle based on the provided display option. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

status.description: The status of the vehicle based on the option.

fuelLevel.description: [Optional] The fuel level of the vehicle in gallons.

batteryVoltage.description: [Optional] The battery voltage of the vehicle in volts.

driver.description: The status of the driver door. [Enum]: ["locked", "unlocked"]

passenger.description: The status of the passenger door. [Enum]: ["locked", "unlocked"]

rear_left.description: The status of the rear left door. [Enum]: ["locked", "unlocked"]

rear_right.description: The status of the rear right door. [Enum]: ["locked", "unlocked"]

currentACTemperature.description: [Optional] The current temperature set in degree Celsius.

fanSpeed.description: [Optional] The fan speed set from 0 to 100.

parkingBrakeForce.description: [Optional] The force applied to the brake in Newtons.

brakePedalStatus.description: [Optional] The status of the brake pedal. [Enum]: ["pressed", "released"]

brakePedalForce.description: [Optional] The force applied to the brake pedal in Newtons.

tools.name: display_log
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Displays the log messages. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

messages.description: The list of messages to display.

tools.name: estimate_distance
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Estimates the distance between two cities. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

cityA.description: The zipcode of the first city.

cityB.description: The zipcode of the second city.

distance.description: The distance between the two cities in km.

intermediaryCities.description: [Optional] The list of intermediary cities between the two cities.

tools.name: estimate_drive_feasibility_by_mileage
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Estimates the milage of the vehicle given the distance needed to drive. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

distance.description: The distance to travel in miles. This is a float type value.

canDrive.description: True if the vehicle can drive the distance, False otherwise.

tools.name: fillFuelTank
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Fills the fuel tank of the vehicle. The fuel tank can hold up to 50 gallons. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

fuelAmount.description: The amount of fuel to fill in gallons; this is the additional fuel to add to the tank. This is a float type value.

fuelLevel.description: The fuel level of the vehicle in gallons.

tools.name: find_nearest_tire_shop
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Finds the nearest tire shop. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

shopLocation.description: The location of the nearest tire shop.

tools.name: gallon_to_liter
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Converts the gallon to liter. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

gallon.description: The amount of gallon to convert. This is a float type value.

tools.name: get_current_speed
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Gets the current speed of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

currentSpeed.description: The current speed of the vehicle in km/h.

tools.name: get_outside_temperature_from_google
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Gets the outside temperature. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

outsideTemperature.description: The outside temperature in degree Celsius.

tools.name: get_outside_temperature_from_weather_com
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Gets the outside temperature. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

outsideTemperature.description: The outside temperature in degree Celsius.

tools.name: get_zipcode_based_on_city
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Gets the zipcode based on the city. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tools.name: liter_to_gallon
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Converts the liter to gallon. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

liter.description: The amount of liter to convert. This is a float type value.

tools.name: lockDoors
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Locks the doors of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

unlock.description: True if the doors are to be unlocked, False otherwise.

door.description: The list of doors to lock or unlock. [Enum]: ["driver", "passenger", "rear_left", "rear_right"]

remainingUnlockedDoors.description: The number of remaining unlocked doors.

tools.name: pressBrakePedal
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Presses the brake pedal based on pedal position. The brake pedal will be kept pressed until released. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

pedalPosition.description: Position of the brake pedal, between 0 (not pressed) and 1 (fully pressed). This is a float type value.

brakePedalStatus.description: The status of the brake pedal. [Enum]: ["pressed", "released"]

brakePedalForce.description: The force applied to the brake pedal in Newtons.

tools.name: releaseBrakePedal
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Releases the brake pedal of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

brakePedalStatus.description: The status of the brake pedal. [Enum]: ["pressed", "released"]

brakePedalForce.description: The force applied to the brake pedal in Newtons.

tools.name: setCruiseControl
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Sets the cruise control of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

speed.description: The speed to set in m/h. The speed should be between 0 and 120 and a multiple of 5. This is a float type value.

activate.description: True to activate the cruise control, False to deactivate.

distanceToNextVehicle.description: The distance to the next vehicle in meters. This is a float type value.

cruiseStatus.description: The status of the cruise control. [Enum]: ["active", "inactive"]

currentSpeed.description: The current speed of the vehicle in km/h.

distanceToNextVehicle.description: The distance to the next vehicle in meters.

tools.name: setHeadlights
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Sets the headlights of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

tools.name: set_navigation
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Navigates to the destination. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

destination.description: The destination to navigate in the format of street, city, state.

tools.name: startEngine
tools.description: This tool belongs to the vehicle control system, which allows users to control various aspects of the car such as engine, doors, climate control, lights, and more. Tool description: Starts the engine of the vehicle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

ignitionMode.description: The ignition mode of the vehicle. [Enum]: ["START", "STOP"]

fuelLevel.description: The fuel level of the vehicle in gallons.

batteryVoltage.description: The battery voltage of the vehicle in volts.

messages.content:
[Golden Rule Compiler]
- For tool-enabled turns, emit the next tool call instead of explanatory prose whenever the next action is already supported by the available tools.
- Do not ask for values that are already explicit in prior user turns, tool outputs, or the current working state.
- Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
- When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
I’m planning a trip that starts from Rivermist, and I need to see if my current fuel situation will get me there. Could you work out the distance?
